keyPoints: Longitudinal multi-arm design (AI-on, AI-off, control; optional hybrid) to test autonomy and dependency over 6–12 months.
Explicit AI-off or fading phases to quantify autonomous performance without AI support.
Standardized engagement dose metrics (frequency, duration, modality, dialog depth) for cross-study comparability.
Co-include both domain-specific skill measures and domain-general problem-solving to assess transfer and autonomy.
Explicit autonomy/dependency outcomes (independence, self-regulated learning, override willingness) and AI-off resilience tasks.
Human-in-the-loop safeguards and ethical governance (explainability, overrides, guardrails) to preserve autonomy and safety.
Pre-registration of hypotheses and analysis plan with effect sizes and confidence intervals for reproducibility.
Incorporation of qualitative methods (think-aloud or interviews) to illuminate autonomy development and perceived dependency.
